questions: Can all seven PDF source documents be read and synthesized accurately into a single cohesive research report?
Does the report follow the narrative-arc structure (Problem -> Insight -> Evidence -> Opportunity -> Ask) prescribed by the implementation plan?
Are all financial figures traceable to source documents with zero discrepancies?
Are production traction metrics accurately reported from verified prior-step values?
Does the capability section describe Helix at the highest category level without software implementation details?
Are branding and positioning lines verbatim from source PDFs?
Does the report include an evidence quality framework with at least three tiers?
Are all four financial stress-test points addressed proactively?

answers.answer: Yes. All seven PDFs (Manifesto, Dovie Offer, Positioning Refined, Positioning Transcript, Reality Check & Risks, One-Pager, Tagline) were read directly and their content synthesized into the report. Prior-step artifacts (product.md, diagnosis-statement.md, tech-research.md, scout-summary.md) provided additional analysis context.
evidence: All seven PDF paths confirmed accessible and read in full
609-line report written to report/report.md synthesizing all sources

answers.answer: Yes. The report follows the prescribed arc: Structural Gap (problem) -> Market Opportunity (size) -> Capabilities (insight) -> Traction (evidence) -> Financial Model (opportunity) -> Competitive Landscape -> Risks -> Team/Investment (ask) -> Branding -> Future Vision. Executive Summary and Evidence Appendix bookend the 10 sections.
evidence: Section headings extracted: 10 numbered sections in prescribed order plus Executive Summary and Appendix

answers.answer: Yes. Five key financial figures verified against Dovie Offer PDF: pre-money valuation ($2.83M), 36-month customer target (999), month-6 profitability threshold (42 customers), 36-month ARR (~$18M calculated from $1,498,500 MRR x 12), conservative TAM ($600M from 10K x $5K x 12). All match source documents exactly.
evidence: Side-by-side comparison performed by verification subagent
Dovie PDF page 1 row 36 confirms 999 customers at month 36
Dovie PDF confirms Month 6 (Sep 26) profitability at 42 customers

answers.answer: Yes. All eight traction metrics match prior-step verified values: organizations (7), users (22), total tickets (264), deployed tickets (124), autonomous rate (81%), configured repos (33), run success rate (55%), NS deployment success rate (31% = 8/26).
evidence: Metrics sourced from scout-summary.md and diagnosis-statement.md
All values cross-checked against report Section 4

answers.answer: Yes. Section 3 describes Helix through six high-level capability categories (autonomous operational lifecycle, governed execution, production inspection, account continuity, bidirectional communication, enterprise security) anchored to Manifesto principles, without software implementation details. No file paths, technology names, pipeline step names, API routes, or code snippets found.
evidence: Verification subagent searched Section 3 for prohibited terms: none found
Word 'diagnosis' used in general English sense, not as pipeline step name
Claude Code reference is in competitive boundary quote, not capability description

answers.answer: Yes. Three key branding lines verified as verbatim matches: 'Owned operations.' matches Tagline PDF finalist #1, 'The ownership layer for NetSuite.' matches Tagline PDF finalist #4, compressed five-line thesis matches Positioning Refined PDF page 3 exactly (all five lines verbatim).
evidence: Side-by-side comparison performed by verification subagent
All five lines of compressed thesis confirmed verbatim match

answers.answer: Yes. The Evidence Sources & Quality appendix includes four tiers: Tier A (hard facts — directly observed), Tier B (well-sourced external data), Tier C (internal projections), Tier D (unverifiable). Each tier includes treatment guidance and examples.
evidence: Appendix section at line 562 contains four-tier framework
Examples assigned to each tier in the framework table

answers.answer: Yes. All four stress-test points addressed proactively in Layer 2 of the Financial Model section: (1) $50K/month flat costs assumption — framed as first-tier economics, (2) zero churn modeling — framed as net-new projection with retention as key metric, (3) zero paying customers today — framed as conversion-ready pipeline, (4) unverifiable Next Technik $60M exit comp — redirected to market-based math.
evidence: Report lines 249-271 contain all four stress-test subsections
Each subsection includes vulnerability description and recommended investor framing

statement: Research report successfully created at report/report.md. The 609-line report synthesizes all seven PDF source documents, six codebases (at the capability category level only), production runtime data, and externally validated market intelligence into a single narrative-arc document with 10 sections optimized for pitch-deck conversion. Updated from prior run to include all seven source documents (adding Positioning Refined, Positioning Transcript, Reality Check, and Tagline), restructured to 10 sections per updated implementation plan, added concentric compression competitive model per tech-research decisions, added milestone-based risk register from Reality Check, added complete branding section from Tagline document, and added evidence quality framework with four tiers. All eight verification checks pass. No code changes were made to any repository.
